title: Day 13 — User Profile & Dynamic Routing
summary: Build dynamic routes with URL parameters, type route params in TypeScript, navigate programmatically, and safely render profile data with optional chaining.

blocks.content: Your app has products, cart, and infinite feeds — but URLs still look static. Production apps are route-driven: /users/42, /products/iphone-15, /orders/abc123. Today you wire URL parameters to React components, fetch profile data by ID, and handle missing users gracefully.

blocks.title: Week 2 Day 6
blocks.content: You learned nested routes on Day 5 and optional chaining on Day 8. Today combines them with dynamic segments. Cart Context from Day 12 should still work across profile routes.

blocks.text: JavaScript — Optional Chaining and Nullish Coalescing
Optional chaining — safe profile field access

blocks.content: User profiles have nullable fields — missing avatar, empty bio, undefined address. Optional chaining (?.) short-circuits to undefined instead of throwing. Nullish coalescing (??) provides fallbacks only for null/undefined, not for 0 or empty string.
Stops evaluation and returns undefined if left side is nullish. Essential for API-driven profile pages before data loads.
user.followers ?? 0 falls back only when null/undefined. user.followers || 0 wrongly replaces 0 followers with 0 anyway — but || replaces 0 with fallback in other cases.

blocks.title: || vs ??
blocks.content: Use ?? for default display values when 0 or '' are valid. Use || when you want falsy values to trigger fallback.
useParams returns string | undefined for each param. Define a Params type and validate before fetch. User model interface matches API shape.

blocks.title: Validate params
blocks.content: URL params are always strings. Parse and validate before API calls. Invalid IDs should route to Not Found — not crash the app.

blocks.text: React Router — Dynamic Routes and Navigation

blocks.content: Colon prefix defines a dynamic segment. /users/:userId matches /users/1 and /users/42 with userId param extracted. Same component renders different data based on URL.
Link renders an anchor that navigates without full page reload — SPA navigation. useNavigate returns a function for programmatic navigation — after form submit, on 404 redirect, or go back.

blocks.title: Deep linking
blocks.content: Users can paste /users/5 directly in the browser. useEffect must fetch on mount when userId param is present — same pattern as Day 08 query-driven fetch.

blocks.text: Build Mission — User Profile & Dynamic Routing

items.text: Loading and error states on profile fetch

blocks.title: Hints
blocks.content: JSONPlaceholder: GET https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users/:id. Invalid IDs return 404 — map to NotFound component. Keep CartProvider above Router so cart works on profile page.
Symptom: userId undefined. Cause: Route uses :id but useParams reads userId. Fix: names must match exactly.
Symptom: Full page reload on click. Cause: <a href='/users/1'>. Fix: React Router Link preserves SPA state including Context.
Symptom: White screen on profile. Cause: user.company.name when company is undefined. Fix: user?.company?.name ?? 'N/A'.
